timingInterpretationPolicy.guidance: When timingInterpretation.selectedTiming.scopeClass is narrow-hot-path, deltaPercent remains a phase-specific diagnostic. Claimability evaluates timingInterpretation.headlineProcessWall.deltaPercent when that end-to-end metric is available.

workloads.name: copy buffer-to-texture contract
workloads.description: Directional copy contract covering buffer-to-texture transfer path.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Directional-only copy contract. This benchmark is intentionally non-claimable until a Dawn-side copy-API parity path is added.

timingNormalization.note: Directional copy contract only: normalize per copy command sequence.

comparabilityCandidate.notes: Promotable once a Dawn map exists for direct copy-path parity.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing measures the comparable operation scope. headlineProcessWall adds process startup and harness overhead for the timed command.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the end-to-end ranking view for one comparable workload unit.

reasons: workload is marked non-comparable by workload contract

workloads.name: copy protocol contract
workloads.description: Directional mixed protocol benchmark combining sampler, copy, barrier, diagnostics, and texture lifecycle checks.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Directional-only protocol contract. This benchmark is intentionally non-claimable while copy-path parity remains directional.

timingNormalization.note: Directional mixed protocol contract only: normalize across one full mixed API sequence.

comparabilityCandidate.notes: Promotable once Dawn benchmark coverage includes the same mixed protocol command sequence.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing measures the comparable operation scope. headlineProcessWall adds process startup and harness overhead for the timed command.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the end-to-end ranking view for one comparable workload unit.
